author: mark
text:
delivered_repo follows the resolved repo on a remote-resolved close

bl-e454 routed populate_on_close's local-miss case through the cross-repo cache, but the bl-7523 auto-tag stayed unconditional: every delivered_in write also set delivered_repo to repo_url::current — the closing clone's origin. On a hub or sibling clone closing a task delivered to another repo, that overwrites the correct delivered_repo with the hub URL, and a later bl show --resolve-remote chases a repo with no [bl-xxxx] tag. The flag meant to recover provenance destroyed it.

resolve_with already reports resolved_repo — the URL the sha was resolved through (repo_url::current on a local hit, the source URL on a remote hit). Thread it through populate_on_close: a resolved sha tags delivered_repo with resolved_repo instead of recomputing repo_url::current. Local hits stay byte-identical; remote hits keep the true source. manual_sha keeps the repo_url::current auto-tag (the operator has the sha locally) and manual_repo still overrides last.
